title: Volfe Karkko
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Character
|type=Sith

|homeworld=[[Anzat/Legends|Anzat]]<ref name="FF137">{{FactFile|137|VOL 1-4|Volfe Karkko}}</ref>
|birth=
|death=[[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]]<ref name="SWR35">[[Star Wars (1998) 35|''Star Wars'' (1998) 35]]</ref> (5),<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Kiffex/Legends|Kiffex]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>
|species=[[Anzat (species)|Anzat]]<ref name="END">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref>
|gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]<ref name="NEC" />

|feathers=

|masters=

}}
{{Quote|Others will come, and then I will feed. Oh, yes. No doubt others will come once they learn Volfe Karkko wakes!|Volfe Karkko|<ref name="SWR32" />}}
'''Volfe Karkko''' was a learned [[Anzat (species)|Anzat]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]–turned–[[Sith sorcerer|Sith magician]] who lived more than a thousand [[Time/Legends|years]] before the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. Karkko believed himself to be above the natural predatory instincts of his [[Species/Legends|species]]; however, after casually tasting the "[[Soup (Anzati)|soup]]," or essence, of several [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings—[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] included—he rapidly developed an addiction which precipitated his fall to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. The threat posed by Karkko's increasing bloodlust caused the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] to rise up in [[Duel on Kiffex|battle]] against him, a fight which the Anzat ultimately lost. Rather than eliminating Karkko, the Council placed him in [[Stasis field/Legends|stasis]] on the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Kiffex/Legends|Kiffex]]. For a millennium, he remained incarcerated, left in full mental consciousness by the Council so that he might contemplate the error of his ways. Karkko, however, refused to renounce the dark side, and spent his time in isolation plotting his revenge against those who had imprisoned him. He became something of a legend among his kind over the years; other Anzati were drawn to Kiffex by Karkko's mental summons, and through their worship at the feet of the man whom they regarded as the '''"Dark Dreamer,"''' his [[Volfe Karkko's temple|prison]] was transformed into a place of hallowed ground. Karkko in turn fed on the essence of his visitors who, over time, devolved into beastly semblances of their former selves.

In [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]], Volfe Karkko [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] the arrival on Kiffex of [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]]—an amnesiac [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]—whom he lured to his prison and tricked into releasing him. Karkko seduced Secura to the dark side and took her as his apprentice, planning to make her his [[Queen/Legends|queen]] upon his escape from Kiffex. He placed his new student in command of his Anzat [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] and sent them to terrorize the populace of Kiffex in an attempt to attract the attention of the Jedi. Several Jedi [[Mission to Kiffex|responded]], but only one, Aayla Secura's former Master [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], actually entered Karkko's stronghold to confront him. When the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi's]] enchantment over Secura was broken by Vos, Karkko attempted to seduce Vos to the dark side, to no avail. Karkko then engaged Vos in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] and was [[Death/Legends|slain]] when the [[Kiffar/Legends|Kiffar]] Jedi Master [[Sai tok/Legends|cleaved him in two]].

==Biography==
===Rare Jedi===
{{Quote|Long, long ago there was an old ''Anzati'' who became a ''Jedi''. His name was ''Volfe Karkko'' and despite the fears of some, he became a ''great'' Jedi.|T'ra Saa|<ref name="SWR34" />}}
Volfe Karkko was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Anzat (species)|Anzat]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Anzat/Legends|Anzat]]<ref name="FF137" /> who lived more than a thousand [[Time/Legends|years]] before the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]].<ref name="SWR35" /> He was already more than eight hundred years old when his [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force sensitivity]] was discovered by a member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]],<ref name="Age">''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' states that Anzati were considered old between 800-951 years of age. ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 137]]'' states that Karkko was already old by the time he started his Jedi training.</ref> and he was selected for [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]], during a time when enrollment was not restricted to children. Karkko eventually became a powerful—but arrogant<ref name="FF137" />—[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]],<ref name="Mastery">[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 137|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 137]] states that Volfe Karkko had access to and engaged in prolonged study of a Sith holocron, a privilege only enjoyed by Jedi Masters as stated in ''[[Legacy of the Jedi]]''.</ref> one whose service to the Order over the years was nothing short of exemplary.<ref name="FF137" /> Karkko was also well known for the excessive amount of time he spent studying one of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Order's rare [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocrons]] kept within the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Temple's]] [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Archives]]. He delighted himself in reminding other Jedi that his people remembered the [[Exiles|first]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]], though even with his extensive scrutiny of the holocron, there appeared to be no actual evidence to substantiate his claim.<ref name="END" />

===Fall to the dark side===
{{Quote|An ''arrogance'' grew in him, however. He had never tasted the 'soup' other Anzati ''savor''—the blend of what you are and what you will be. He believed he was ''beyond'' that addiction, having never tasted it…so he told himself there could be no harm in tasting it ''once''. And in his pride he fell. He used his Jedi powers to harvest more soup, taking even other Jedi, and so he came to the dark side.|T'ra Saa|<ref name="SWR34" />}}
[[File:VolfeFalls-Darkness.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Volfe Karkko battles the Jedi Council on Kiffex.]]
Some members of the Jedi Order, among them the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] itself, believed that training Anzati to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] was a dangerous affair,<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> and there were fears that Volfe Karkko would not be able to resist his [[Species/Legends|species]]' instinctive need to taste the "[[Soup (Anzati)|soup]]," or life energy, of other [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]]. Karkko vehemently dismissed these concerns, as his pride led him to believe that succumbing to such afflictions was beneath a Jedi as accomplished as him. He reasoned that because he had never tasted soup before, a single experience would be harmless. Upon his first taste, however, Karkko immediately became addicted, and craved soup ever thereafter.<ref name="FF137" /> With his [[Force power/Legends|Jedi abilities]], he began preying on other members of the Order,<ref name="SWR34" /> and through consumption of their essence Karkko became stronger in the Force.<ref name="CSWE" /> The Anzat fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] as a result and believed that he could single-handedly conquer [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="FF137" />

Karkko's ambitions were deemed a threat to the Order, and the Jedi Council was forced to intervene. The Anzat fled to the world [[Kiffex/Legends|Kiffex]] in an effort to escape persecution, but he was still pursued by the Council. A [[Duel on Kiffex|battle]] ensued when they found him, during which Karkko held his own for a time. He was ultimately defeated by the Councilors who, rather than [[Death/Legends|kill]] him, [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] him in a [[Stasis field/Legends|stasis field]]. Karkko was unable to move but remained in full consciousness, sentenced to a period of solitary confinement and left to reflect on his transgressions. Karkko's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] was placed nearby in a miniature stasis field to serve both as a reminder to him of his former Jedi life, and as a warning to others of what could also befall them. The building on Kiffex which served as Karkko's prison was sealed to prevent anyone other than Jedi from gaining access.<ref name="SWR34" />

The memory of Volfe Karkko's downfall remained prominent throughout his one-thousand-[[Galactic Standard Calendar|year]] imprisonment. He achieved legendary status among the Anzat people<ref name="VosDB">{{DB|character|quinlanvos|Vos, Quinlan}}</ref> as well as the Jedi, and his tale was even recorded in a holocron which remained locked away in the [[Holocron Chamber]] of the Jedi Temple's [[Tower of First Knowledge/Legends|Tower of First Knowledge]].<ref name="SWR33">[[Star Wars (1998) 33|''Star Wars'' (1998) 33]]</ref> Karkko's perpetual isolation on Kiffex did nothing to sway his allegiance to the dark side, however. While in solitude, he spent his time plotting the downfall of the Jedi Order and the resources such an aspiration required.<ref name="SWR34" /> As the centuries passed, Karkko's prison became a place of concentrated dark side power.<ref name="CSWE" /> His conscious spirit [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] called to other Anzati and lured them to Kiffex. They were able to infiltrate his [[Volfe Karkko's temple|prison]], where they worshiped at the feet of the one whom they called "The Dreaming One." He corrupted them with his power, and by mentally draining the essence of his supplicants, Karkko was able to replenish his own strength. The [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] soon had an [[Army/Legends|army]] of feral Anzat [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]], utterly devoid of sentience and completely bound to his will.<ref name="SWR34" /> None of the sycophants were able to release Karkko from the stasis field, something only another Jedi was capable of.<ref name="FF137" />

===Freedom===
{{Quote|Volfe Karkko must ''not'' be allowed to escape Kiffex. No matter what the cost.|Mace Windu|<ref name="SWR34" />}}
[[File:Volfe_Karkko_stasis_field.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Aayla Secura discovers Volfe Karkko.]]
In [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]], after a thousand years in stasis, Volfe Karkko was finally granted an opportunity to escape confinement when a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] named [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]] arrived on Kiffex. Although Secura was suffering from amnesia at the time, one particular memory remained with her: the murder of [[Pol Secura|her uncle]] at the hands of her own Jedi Master, [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]]. It was through her anger at Vos and desire for revenge that Volfe Karkko [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] Secura's presence and began corrupting her to the dark side. He telepathically called her to his domicile and persuaded her to release him from stasis. Initially tempted with consuming the soup of a Jedi in such close proximity to him, Karkko resisted the urge to sup on her brain matter despite the deprivation he had long been forced to endure.<ref name="FF137" /> He instead took Secura as his apprentice,<ref name="Apprentice" /> with plans of eventually making her his [[Queen/Legends|queen]] after he abandoned Kiffex and had exacted revenge against the Jedi Order.<ref name="SWR34" />

Karkko immediately began preparing for his escape from Kiffex and saw his army of degenerate Anzati, led by Aayla Secura, as the means by which he would do so. He therefore placed her in command of his slaves and ordered them to attack a nearby security facility, where they slaughtered everyone inside. This attracted the attention of [[Sheyf]] [[Tinté Vos]], ruler of Kiffex,<ref name="FF137" /> who contacted the Jedi Council and requested that her nephew Quinlan be [[Mission to Kiffex|dispatched]] to investigate the disaster.<ref name="SWR33" />

During this same time, [[Astaal Vilbum]], the [[Caretaker of First Knowledge/Legends|Caretaker]] of the Jedi Temple's Tower of First Knowledge, witnessed the independent activation of the holocron which contained the legend of Volfe Karkko—one that had remained undisturbed for nearly fourteen lifetimes. Vilbum was greatly troubled by this omen and alerted fellow Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], the Councilor who had assented to Quinlan Vos' intervention on Kiffex. The holocron's activation signified Volfe Karkko's release from stasis, and both Vilbum and Windu realized that Vos—who had a deep-seated fear of Anzati—was walking into a trap.<ref name="SWR33" />

===Consolidation of power===
{{Quote|I will drink the essence of these ''other'' Jedi and then we will escape this tomb of a planet…and I will make you my ''queen''.|Volfe Karkko, explaining his plans to Aayla Secura|<ref name="SWR34" />}}
A pack of Volfe Karkko's slaves found and attacked Quinlan Vos and fellow Master [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] when they landed on Kiffex, but were quickly slain by the Jedi.<ref name="SWR32" /> Another group of Anzati, this time led by Aayla Secura, was sent out to capture sentient beings for Karkko so that he might consume their essence. They attacked the patrons of the [[Black Hole (Kiffex)|Black Hole cantina]], but were opposed by Vos, Tholme, and a third Jedi, the [[Veknoid/Legends|Veknoid]] Master [[Zao]]. While Karkko's slaves harvested from the patrons of the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], his Twi'lek apprentice briefly fought her former Master, until a telepathic suggestion from Karkko reminded her that the mission he had sent her on superseded her need for vengeance. Secura, along with Karkko's Anzat minions, then fled the cantina, but the Jedi were in fast pursuit. After gaining a significant lead on them, Secura ordered the Anzati to hide themselves and ambush the Jedi when they passed, making sure to take them alive if possible so that Karkko could feast upon their soup. The subsequent attack was also repelled, thanks to the intervention of Kiffex's [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Jedi Watchman]], Master [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]].<ref name="SWR33" />

Karkko was joined by Secura shortly afterward and listened as she reported her inability to complete the missions he had given her. He acknowledged her failure but explained that it had been his will that compelled Secura to abandon her task. Karkko mused over the fact that the consumption of a Jedi's soup would greatly increase his strength, prompting his apprentice to offer her own essence to him. The Anzat refused,<ref name="SWR34" /> preferring to instead wait for the Jedi whom he knew would most certainly come to him.<ref name="VosDB" />

===Final battle===
{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''Welcome'' to my sanctum. For a ''millennia'', my ''prison''. Now my throne. Outside of my ''apprentice'', you are my first ''guest''.|Volfe Karkko greets Quinlan Vos|<ref name="SWR35" />}}
[[File:Volfe_Karkko_death.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Karkko is killed by Quinlan Vos.]]
Karkko patiently anticipated the four Jedi Masters who approached his fortress and did battle with the Anzat defenders outside. They were able to defeat Karkko's slaves, but only one of the Jedi, Quinlan Vos, proceeded further into Karkko's domain. The Anzat was with Secura, Vos' former Padawan, in the inner sanctum when Vos arrived. Karkko greeted him before ordering the Twi'lek to attack. Secura dueled with Vos for a short time before the Jedi Master was able to break Karkko's spell over her by refusing to fight any longer. Enraged by the turn of events, Karkko blasted and incapacitated Secura with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], and then turned his attention to Vos.<ref name="SWR35" />

Karkko ignited his own weapon and engaged Vos in lightsaber combat while [[Dun Möch/Legends|taunting]] Vos and attempting to lure him to the dark side. Vos tried to cut Karkko down after a brief clash of [[blade]]s, but the Dark Jedi vanished into thin air and simultaneously conjured [[Mind trick/Legends#Force Illusion|illusions]] of monstrous beasts to confound his opponent. Karkko then appeared behind Vos and used the Force to [[Telekinesis/Legends|slam]] him into a nearby wall. He pressed his advantage but was halted when Vos unleashed a torrent of Force lightning, an ability the Anzat had not expected a proponent of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]] to employ. Stalled only momentarily, Karkko projected his own lightning, the strength of which disabled Vos long enough for Karkko to prepare to harvest his soup. Contrarily, the Anzat neglected to account for Vos' companions outside the fortress—Masters Tholme, T'ra Saa, and Zao—whose combined power in the Force [[Force valor|gave Vos enough strength]] to break Karkko's telepathic hold over him. Recovering from Vos' retaliatory assault, Karkko saluted his opponent with his lightsaber and readied himself for another exchange, only to be killed immediately afterward when Vos [[Sai tok/Legends|cut him in two]].<ref name="SWR35" />

===Legacy===
The legend of Volfe Karkko and his fall to the dark side of the Force survived for many years after his death.<ref name="END" /> The Jedi–turned–[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], [[Head of State/Legends|leader]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]],<ref name="AOTC">{{Film|II}}</ref> came into possession of one of Karkko's precious [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] holocrons, and used it in his seduction of another Anzat Jedi named [[Nikkos Tyris]]. Karkko had been something of a role model for Tyris, who painstakingly studied the teachings of his predecessor. Tempted by Dooku's promise of power, Tyris fell to the dark side and abandoned the Jedi Order as Karkko had done before him. He believed that he had discovered the ''"[[Saarai]],"'' or ''"True Way,"''<ref name="END" /> and it was upon this belief that the foundations of the [[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]] Order of Force-users was founded.<ref name="IJ">''[[I, Jedi]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I allow no traitors.|Volfe Karkko|<ref name="SWR35" />}}
Volfe Karkko, whose given name was a common occurrence among the Anzati,<ref name="TUR">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref> stood nearly two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall<ref name="FF137" /> and clad himself in flowing, [[black]] [[Dark Jedi robe|robes]]. He had olive skin and long black hair that he kept bound at the back of his head. Karkko also maintained a slight mustache and goatee.<ref name="SWR34" /> Like all members of the Anzat race,<ref name="NEGAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> Volfe Karkko possessed two tentacle-like proboscises hidden within his cheeks. These appendages only revealed themselves when the Dark Jedi was ready to feed on the soup of a sentient being.<ref name="SWR35" /> He spoke his native [[Anzat (language)|Anzat language]] as well as [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]].<ref name="Bilingual">''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' states that all Anzati spoke both their native language and Galactic Basic Standard.</ref>

[[File:AaylaKaarko-SWREP34.png|thumb|right|200px|Volfe Karkko with his would-be consort, Aayla Secura.]]
Volfe Karkko served the Order with distinction during his time as a Jedi. He was faulted by his peers, however, for what they perceived as his proud and extremely arrogant attitude.<ref name="FF137" /> He was well known throughout the Order for his prolonged study of a rare Sith holocron, and he often boasted about how the Anzati knew of the original Sith Lords, a claim that was never proven. The teachings of Volfe Karkko regarding the Sith, which he compiled from various sources of Sith lore, were considered unorthodox by his Jedi peers.<ref name="END" /> Karkko felt he was superior to the compelling nature of soup consumption and cited his Jedi training as the reason why such shortcomings had no affect on him. His first indulgence was based on that belief and resulted in a subsequent addiction. Over time, he developed a preference for other Jedi to satisfy his cravings. These acts, combined with his immense pride and brazen demeanor, paved Karkko's path to the dark side of the Force.<ref name="SWR34" />

The Jedi Council hoped that Karkko would reflect on the his fall to the dark side when he was apprehended and placed in stasis.<ref name="SWR34" /> On the contrary, Karkko intended to do no such thing and instead spent the centuries both nursing his hatred of the Jedi Order and contemplating his revenge against them.<ref name="FF137" /> By 30 BBY, the fear of Karkko's freedom was still a cause of distress among the members of the current Jedi Council, and preventing his escape was a priority of the highest order.<ref name="SWR35" /> By the time Karkko was set free, his primary concerns included tasting soup<ref name="SWR32" /> and leaving Kiffex.<ref name="FF137" /> He was content with delaying his soup craving for the more important escape, a plan he enacted upon his release. Karkko desired the essence of a Jedi to satisfy his craving,<ref name="SWR34" /> and he anticipated the arrival of those whom he was certain would respond once they became aware of his new-found freedom. Karkko was angered by the treachery of Aayla Secura, whom he considered a turncoat after his spell over her had been broken by Quinlan Vos. Karkko immobilized Secura without hesitation once he determined that she would no longer serve his purposes. He was intrigued by the darkness he sensed within Vos, but after several attempts, Karkko was unable to convince the Jedi Master to embrace it.<ref name="SWR35" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|Force lightning! Only one who has allied with the ''dark side'' supposedly can call on it. Have you crossed that line, pitiful Jedi? You are a ''novice'' with it. I am a ''master''.|Volfe Karkko, to Quinlan Vos|<ref name="SWR35" />}}
[[File:VolfeKarkkoLightsaber-SW1998-32.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Volfe Karkko's lightsaber]]
While the Anzati had natural telepathic abilities with which they subdued their prey, Volfe Karkko's Jedi training afforded him a greater aptitude with mental manipulation. The Jedi took precautionary measures and developed methods of resisting the Anzati's strong hypnotic powers,<ref name="FF137" /> but Karkko learned ways to counter these techniques.<ref name="SWR35" /> In draining the essence of Jedi, Karkko's benefits were twofold: his innate hunger was satisfied while he also grew stronger in the Force.<ref name="CSWE" />

Karkko was very strong in the Force,<ref name="FF137" /> and learned in the ways of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]].<ref name="TSEUC">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030814trasaa|text=T'ra Saa}}</ref> By the time he had fallen to the dark side, he was considered to be so much of a threat that the Jedi Council personally pursued him. Indeed, he had succeeded in slaying several of the Councilors before they were finally able to subdue him. While in stasis on Kiffex, Karkko's dark side aura permeated first his prison,<ref name="CSWE" /> and then the planet itself. He was still able to manipulate the Force and used it as the medium through which he summoned members of his species from as far as Anzat to his prison. Continuous exposure to Karkko's telepathic influence eroded their sentience, and with time, he bound all of them to his will.<ref name="SWR34" /> Karkko subjected Aayla Secura to the same treatment upon her arrival on Kiffex, taking advantage of her fragile mental state in order to convert the amnesiac Jedi to his service.<ref name="FF137" /> Aside from telepathy, Karkko was skilled with Force lightning,<ref name="SWR34" /> an ability over which he touted total mastery. Casting Force illusions and disappearing into thin air also fell within the scope of his powers. He was proficient with telekinesis, using it to both slam his opponents into walls as well as paralyzing them just prior to consuming their soup.<ref name="SWR35" />

Karkko owned a [[red]]-bladed lightsaber of his own construction<ref name="SWR32" /> and wielded it in tandem with his Force powers. When dueling, Karkko switched between a single-handed, reverse grip<ref name="SWR34" /> and a traditional two-handed style.<ref name="SWR35" /> Prior to his incarceration, his prowess was such that he was capable of fighting multiple members of the Jedi Council simultaneously. It was thus that he would have defeated and killed Quinlan Vos then, were it not for the mental interference of three Jedi Masters.<ref name="SWR34" />

==Behind the scenes==
Volfe Karkko first appeared as the primary antagonist of the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' story arc [[Star Wars: Darkness|''Darkness'']], written by [[John Ostrander]] and penciled by artist [[Jan Duursema]]. Karkko is mentioned in connection to other ''[[Star Wars]]'' characters in several pieces of source material, such as ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', [[Star Wars Insider 87|''Star Wars Insider'' 87]]{{'s}} ''[[Order 66: Destroy All Jedi]]'', and [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]. He received further mention in [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entries for both Aayla Secura and Quinlan Vos. "T'ra Saa: An Expanded Universe Character" first identifies him as a [[Sith sorcerer|Sith magician]], a distinction later corroborated by Saa's entry in [[2008]]'s [[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia|''Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'']]. 

Several contradictions arise when comparing the ''Star Wars: Darkness'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]] to both [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 137|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 137]] and ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia''. In ''Star Wars: Republic 35: Darkness, Part 4'', Volfe Karkko is shown holding his lightsaber when he is cut in half by Quinlan Vos. ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 137]]'', however, states that Vos used Karkko's own lightsaber to kill him. ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' goes on to say that Karkko enthralled the local Anzat populace of Kiffex, but both the ''Official Star Wars Fact File 137'' and ''Star Wars: Republic 34: Darkness, Part 3'' state that the Anzati were drawn to Kiffex from their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] through Karkko's telepathic summons.
